nodes.label: the verb surface
WHEN `revitify --help` runs THEN it SHALL list build/query/explain/path/affected/communities/ export/watch/global/prs/validate; IF an unknown verb is given THEN exit code SHALL be 1.

nodes.id: test:test/cli.test.ts::help lists every verb; unknown verb exits 1
nodes.label: test/cli.test.ts::help lists every verb; unknown verb exits 1
verbs work end to end on a real project
WHEN build/query/path/affected/communities/export/validate run in sequence THEN each SHALL exit 0 with its artifact/output, and the JSONL query log SHALL record the session.

nodes.id: test:test/cli.test.ts::build → query → path → affected → communities → export → validate, end to end
nodes.label: test/cli.test.ts::build → query → path → affected → communities → export → validate, end to end
watch, global, prs

WHEN watch runs THEN it SHALL rebuild incrementally on change (cache-backed); WHEN global runs over N repos THEN ids SHALL prefix `repo:` (collision-proof); WHEN prs runs in a git repo THEN changed files SHALL map to their transitive blast radius.

nodes.id: test:test/cli.test.ts::rebuilds on change (initial build, then incremental)
nodes.label: test/cli.test.ts::rebuilds on change (initial build, then incremental)

nodes.id: test:test/cli.test.ts::global merges repos with repo: prefixes
nodes.label: test/cli.test.ts::global merges repos with repo: prefixes

nodes.id: test:test/cli.test.ts::prs reports diff impact in a git repo
nodes.label: test/cli.test.ts::prs reports diff impact in a git repo
the three artifacts, default revitify-out/
WHEN `revitify(rootDir)` runs on any project THEN the system SHALL write `graph.json`, `graph.html` (self-contained, no remote scripts), and `GRAPH_REPORT.md` into `<rootDir>/revitify-out/`.

nodes.id: test:test/revitify.test.ts::emits graph.json + self-contained graph.html + GRAPH_REPORT.md (default: revitify-out/)
nodes.label: test/revitify.test.ts::emits graph.json + self-contained graph.html + GRAPH_REPORT.md (default: revitify-out/)
Rivet's call shape stays supported forever

WHEN `revitify(rootDir, "graphify-out")` runs (the exact call Rivet's `refreshCodeGraphVia` makes) THEN the system SHALL write the same three artifacts into `<rootDir>/graphify-out/` and every symbol node SHALL carry exactly `id, name, label, kind, source_file, source_location, community`.

nodes.id: test:test/revitify.test.ts::keeps Rivet's call shape working: revitify(dir, 'graphify-out')
nodes.label: test/revitify.test.ts::keeps Rivet's call shape working: revitify(dir, 'graphify-out')
graph fields are additive-only

WHEN the graph is built THEN nodes SHALL carry `id`, `label`, `source_file`, `community` and links SHALL carry `source`, `target`, `relation`, and the graph SHALL carry `built_at_commit` when the project is a git checkout; new fields MAY be added but these SHALL never be renamed or removed.

nodes.id: test:test/revitify.test.ts::builds containment, import, and reference edges with source_file + community on nodes
nodes.label: test/revitify.test.ts::builds containment, import, and reference edges with source_file + community on nodes

nodes.id: test:test/contract.test.ts::a canonical symbol node carries exactly the contract fields
nodes.label: test/contract.test.ts::a canonical symbol node carries exactly the contract fields

Scenario: a contract-breaking shape fails loudly, not silently (negative floor) — Given a graph.json whose nodes lost the `source_file` field When the `assertGraphContract` validator (src/model/contract.ts) checks it Then validation throws naming the offending node index and field

nodes.id: test:test/contract.test.ts::contract drift fails loudly, naming index and field (negative floor)
nodes.label: test/contract.test.ts::contract drift fails loudly, naming index and field (negative floor)
refactors never change output bytes

WHEN the engine is restructured without intended behavior change (Phase 1a modularization) THEN graph.json on the committed fixture SHALL be byte-identical to the committed expectation, and the type-level pin against Rivet's reader SHALL keep compiling.

nodes.id: test:test/contract.test.ts::graph.json is byte-identical to the committed expectation (refactor pin)
nodes.label: test/contract.test.ts::graph.json is byte-identical to the committed expectation (refactor pin)

nodes.id: test:test/contract.test-d.ts::RevitifyGraph stays assignable to the shape Rivet's loadCodeGraph reads
nodes.label: test/contract.test-d.ts::RevitifyGraph stays assignable to the shape Rivet's loadCodeGraph reads
symbol→symbol call edges

WHEN a callable's body calls another in-project symbol THEN a `calls` edge SHALL connect the two symbols (not their files), confidence-tagged by resolution; built-ins and externals SHALL leave no edge.
test/intelligence.test.ts::symbol→symbol calls edges, confidence-tagged
test/intelligence.test.ts::unresolvable callees (built-ins) leave no edge
tiered resolution precedence

WHEN a name has multiple candidate definitions THEN resolution SHALL prefer same-file, then same-directory, then global; a unique candidate in the winning tier is INFERRED, ties within it are AMBIGUOUS with a lexicographic pick.

nodes.id: test:test/intelligence.test.ts::same-file beats same-dir beats global; unique-in-tier is INFERRED
nodes.label: test/intelligence.test.ts::same-file beats same-dir beats global; unique-in-tier is INFERRED

nodes.id: test:test/intelligence.test.ts::ties within the winning tier are AMBIGUOUS with lexicographic pick
nodes.label: test/intelligence.test.ts::ties within the winning tier are AMBIGUOUS with lexicographic pick
near-duplicate doc nodes merge; code never does

WHEN two doc nodes' normalized labels are minhash-near (≥0.7) AND Jaro-Winkler-confirmed (≥0.92) THEN they SHALL merge into the first-seen node with links rewritten and exact (source, target, relation) duplicates dropped; identical CODE labels SHALL never merge.

nodes.id: test:test/intelligence.test.ts::merges near-duplicate headings, rewrites links, never touches code symbols
nodes.label: test/intelligence.test.ts::merges near-duplicate headings, rewrites links, never touches code symbols

nodes.id: test:test/algorithms.test.ts::no docs / single doc: nothing merges, exact duplicate links still drop
nodes.label: test/algorithms.test.ts::no docs / single doc: nothing merges, exact duplicate links still drop
structural communities, deterministic

WHEN the graph clusters THEN dense subgraphs SHALL separate at their bridges (Louvain + connectivity refinement), isolated nodes SHALL keep singleton communities, oversized communities (>25%, ≥10) SHALL re-split, and two runs SHALL produce identical assignments.

nodes.id: test:test/intelligence.test.ts::separates the two clusters; deterministic across runs
nodes.label: test/intelligence.test.ts::separates the two clusters; deterministic across runs

nodes.id: test:test/intelligence.test.ts::isolated nodes get their own communities
nodes.label: test/intelligence.test.ts::isolated nodes get their own communities

nodes.id: test:test/algorithms.test.ts::an oversized clique triggers the re-split pass and survives unsplit (no substructure)
nodes.label: test/algorithms.test.ts::an oversized clique triggers the re-split pass and survives unsplit (no substructure)

Scenario: a structureless giant cannot be force-split (negative floor) — Given a 12-node clique holding >25% of the graph When the oversized re-split runs Louvain on its subgraph Then the clique stays one community — re-splitting requires real substructure, never fiat
registry dispatch, not switches

WHEN a file is ingested THEN the system SHALL pick its extractor via the ordered registration array (extension pre-filter, then detect()), and WHEN no registration matches (e.g. `.d.ts`, unknown extensions) THEN the system SHALL skip the file rather than fail.

nodes.id: test:test/layers.test.ts::dispatches by detect/extensions and rejects .d.ts and unknown files
nodes.label: test/layers.test.ts::dispatches by detect/extensions and rejects .d.ts and unknown files
the lazy boundary
WHEN a registration provides only `load()` (a heavy/lazy module) THEN `resolveSync` SHALL return undefined (sync facade skips it) and `resolve()` SHALL load it once and memoize.

nodes.id: test:test/layers.test.ts::resolves lazily (async), memoizes, and resolveSync returns undefined without loadSync
nodes.label: test/layers.test.ts::resolves lazily (async), memoizes, and resolveSync returns undefined without loadSync

Scenario: a forbidden cross-layer import fails CI, not code review (negative floor) — Given a source file in src/model/ importing from src/export/ When `pnpm depcruise` runs (the layer matrix in .dependency-cruiser.cjs) Then the build fails naming the violating edge And the same gate forbids circular imports, orphans, and node:* inside src/model/

nodes.id: test:test/boundaries.test.ts::a forbidden src/model → src/export import fails depcruise, naming the violated rule
nodes.label: test/boundaries.test.ts::a forbidden src/model → src/export import fails depcruise, naming the violated rule
deep member extraction per language

WHEN a Java file is extracted via tree-sitter THEN classes, interfaces, enums, records, methods, **constructors, and fields** SHALL each become symbols (members as `sym:<rel>#<Type>.<member>`), and WHEN Python/Go/Rust files are extracted THEN classes/functions/methods, funcs/methods/types, and fns/structs/enums/traits/impl-methods SHALL respectively become symbols.

nodes.id: test:test/multilang.test.ts::java: constructors, methods, fields, nested types — full member depth
nodes.label: test/multilang.test.ts::java: constructors, methods, fields, nested types — full member depth

nodes.id: test:test/multilang.test.ts::python: classes, nested methods, functions, file-resolved imports
nodes.label: test/multilang.test.ts::python: classes, nested methods, functions, file-resolved imports

nodes.id: test:test/multilang.test.ts::go and rust symbols, and the whole graph passes the contract
nodes.label: test/multilang.test.ts::go and rust symbols, and the whole graph passes the contract

IF a Python `from a.b import c` is extracted THEN the system SHALL reference only the imported names — never the module-path tail (the `current === moduleNode` wrapper-identity bug fabricated a `name:b` reference).

nodes.id: test:test/multilang.test.ts::'from pkg.helpers import slugify' references slugify only — never a phantom 'helpers'
nodes.label: test/multilang.test.ts::'from pkg.helpers import slugify' references slugify only — never a phantom 'helpers'
the sync facade is frozen and deterministic

WHEN `buildGraph()` (sync) runs THEN tree-sitter SHALL NOT load — regex fallbacks serve py/java, go/rust are skipped — and an earlier `buildGraphAsync` in the same process SHALL NOT change the sync output (no cache-leak upgrades).

nodes.id: test:test/multilang.test.ts::falls back to regex (shallow py/java, no go/rust) — never loads tree-sitter
nodes.label: test/multilang.test.ts::falls back to regex (shallow py/java, no go/rust) — never loads tree-sitter

nodes.id: test:test/lazy-boundary.test.ts::sync revitify never resolves web-tree-sitter or grammar packages
nodes.label: test/lazy-boundary.test.ts::sync revitify never resolves web-tree-sitter or grammar packages

Scenario: the lazy-boundary proof cannot silently rot (negative floor) — Given the module-resolution recorder hook in a child process When the async path runs on a Python file Then the log DOES contain web-tree-sitter — proving the hook records before we trust its silence

nodes.id: test:test/lazy-boundary.test.ts::positive control: the async path DOES resolve web-tree-sitter through the same hook
nodes.label: test/lazy-boundary.test.ts::positive control: the async path DOES resolve web-tree-sitter through the same hook
per-file cache with honest invalidation

WHEN a build re-runs unchanged THEN every file SHALL be a cache hit (stat fastpath, no re-read); WHEN one file changes THEN exactly that file SHALL re-extract; WHEN the file SET changes THEN the whole cache SHALL invalidate (fragments embed import resolution against the walked set); IF the index corrupts or fragments are evicted THEN the build SHALL recover, never crash.

nodes.id: test:test/cache.test.ts::second run is all hits; edits invalidate exactly one file; output identical
nodes.label: test/cache.test.ts::second run is all hits; edits invalidate exactly one file; output identical

nodes.id: test:test/cache.test.ts::adding a file invalidates the set (import resolution depends on the walked set)
nodes.label: test/cache.test.ts::adding a file invalidates the set (import resolution depends on the walked set)

nodes.id: test:test/cache.test.ts::recovers from a corrupt stat-index and from evicted fragment files
nodes.label: test/cache.test.ts::recovers from a corrupt stat-index and from evicted fragment files
parallel extraction changes nothing but wall-clock
WHEN worker-pool extraction runs THEN the graph SHALL be byte-identical to the sequential run (fragments reassemble in walk order).
test/lazy-boundary.test.ts::worker-pool output matches sequential output exactly
the offline gate is absolute

WHEN no API key and no local tool is present THEN a build over code+PDF+audio SHALL produce a graph identical to the code-only build, make ZERO network calls, and print one skip notice per gated ingestor.

nodes.id: test:test/multimodal.test.ts::GATE: without keys the run never touches the network and equals the code-only graph
nodes.label: test/multimodal.test.ts::GATE: without keys the run never touches the network and equals the code-only graph
backend autodetection mirrors llm.py
WHEN keys are present THEN detection SHALL order Anthropic → Gemini → OpenAI → Ollama, each backend speaking its provider's response shape over fetch, erroring loudly on non-OK.
test/multimodal.test.ts::anthropic → gemini → openai → ollama → none

nodes.id: test:test/multimodal.test.ts::gemini, openai, ollama parse their shapes; anthropic errors loudly on non-ok
nodes.label: test/multimodal.test.ts::gemini, openai, ollama parse their shapes; anthropic errors loudly on non-ok

nodes.id: test:test/multimodal.test.ts::with a key + mocked backend, PDFs contribute concept nodes
nodes.label: test/multimodal.test.ts::with a key + mocked backend, PDFs contribute concept nodes
offline schema/deps ingestion
WHEN .sql DDL or Cargo.toml is walked THEN tables/columns/REFERENCES and crate/dependency edges SHALL extract fully offline (both pipelines — these ingestors are sync-capable).

nodes.id: test:test/multimodal.test.ts::tables, columns, and REFERENCES edges from DDL
nodes.label: test/multimodal.test.ts::tables, columns, and REFERENCES edges from DDL
test/multimodal.test.ts::crate + dependency edges from Cargo.toml
local tools never become hard dependencies

WHEN whisper-cli/scip exist on PATH THEN AV/SCIP files SHALL ingest through them; IF the binary is absent or fails THEN the ingestor SHALL report unavailable / return empty — never throw, never install anything.

nodes.id: test:test/multimodal.test.ts::whisper + scip spawn, parse, and survive failures
nodes.label: test/multimodal.test.ts::whisper + scip spawn, parse, and survive failures
test/multimodal.test.ts::detect + available are honest
path, neighborhood, affected

WHEN two symbols connect THEN `shortestPath` SHALL return the BFS path; WHEN a symbol changes THEN `affected` SHALL return its transitive reverse dependents (imports/calls/references, hopping containers); IF endpoints are missing THEN path SHALL be undefined, never a throw.

nodes.id: test:test/query.test.ts::finds the shortest path between symbols across files
nodes.label: test/query.test.ts::finds the shortest path between symbols across files
test/query.test.ts::walks reverse dependencies transitively
search, explain, communities, log

WHEN a question is asked THEN nodes SHALL rank by idf-weighted term match and `explain` SHALL narrate seeds with neighbors; communities SHALL report cohesion in [0,1]; every query SHALL append a JSONL log entry that never breaks the query on failure.

nodes.id: test:test/query.test.ts::idf-ranks matches and explains with neighbors
nodes.label: test/query.test.ts::idf-ranks matches and explains with neighbors

nodes.id: test:test/query.test.ts::lists communities with size and cohesion in [0,1]
nodes.label: test/query.test.ts::lists communities with size and cohesion in [0,1]
test/query.test.ts::appends JSONL entries
the graphify export family

WHEN extra exports run THEN callflow.html SHALL hold only `calls` edges, tree.html SHALL nest directories/files/symbols, WIKI.md SHALL section per community, graph.mmd SHALL cap at the top symbols; IF the graph is empty THEN every exporter SHALL render without throwing.

nodes.id: test:test/query.test.ts::callflow keeps only calls; tree nests files; wiki sections communities; mermaid caps
nodes.label: test/query.test.ts::callflow keeps only calls; tree nests files; wiki sections communities; mermaid caps
test/query.test.ts::exporters survive an empty graph
why-nodes

WHEN a comment carries NOTE:/WHY:/HACK: THEN it SHALL become a why node linked (`explains`) from the symbol declared just under it (file fallback), and WHEN a Python definition opens with a docstring THEN a docstring node SHALL hang off that symbol (`documents`).

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::typescript: NOTE/WHY/HACK comments become nodes explained by the next symbol
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::typescript: NOTE/WHY/HACK comments become nodes explained by the next symbol

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::python: docstrings and HACK comments via tree-sitter
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::python: docstrings and HACK comments via tree-sitter
god nodes are symbols, not containers
WHEN god nodes rank THEN file/doc/why/docstring nodes SHALL be excluded.

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::ranks symbols only — file/doc/why nodes are excluded
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::ranks symbols only — file/doc/why nodes are excluded
the report carries its intelligence
WHEN GRAPH_REPORT.md renders THEN it SHALL include god nodes, surprising connections with reasons, a confidence summary, why-nodes when present, and at least 4 suggested questions.

nodes.id: test:test/report.test.ts::carries god nodes, surprises, confidence, why-nodes, and ≥4 questions
nodes.label: test/report.test.ts::carries god nodes, surprises, confidence, why-nodes, and ≥4 questions
test/report.test.ts::suggestedQuestions seeds from brokers and ambiguity
every edge carries its confidence

WHEN the graph is built THEN structural contains/imports edges SHALL carry `confidence: "EXTRACTED"`, and WHEN a `name:` reference resolves to exactly one symbol THEN the edge SHALL carry `confidence: "INFERRED"`.
test/resolution.test.ts::structural contains/imports edges are EXTRACTED
test/resolution.test.ts::a uniquely-resolved reference is INFERRED
ambiguity is tagged, never hidden

WHEN a reference has multiple candidate definitions THEN the system SHALL resolve through tiers (same-file, then same-directory, then global); a candidate that is UNIQUE in the winning tier is tagged `confidence: "INFERRED"`, while ties WITHIN a tier SHALL be picked deterministically (lexicographic id, never insertion order) and tagged `confidence: "AMBIGUOUS"`.

nodes.id: test:test/resolution.test.ts::same-dir tier disambiguates: unique-in-tier resolves INFERRED (Phase 3 precedence)
nodes.label: test/resolution.test.ts::same-dir tier disambiguates: unique-in-tier resolves INFERRED (Phase 3 precedence)

nodes.id: test:test/resolution.test.ts::falls back to lexicographic id order, still AMBIGUOUS
nodes.label: test/resolution.test.ts::falls back to lexicographic id order, still AMBIGUOUS

nodes.id: test:test/resolution.test.ts::the pick is stable across runs
nodes.label: test/resolution.test.ts::the pick is stable across runs
imports resolve against reality
WHEN a relative import spec is extracted THEN the system SHALL resolve it against the walked-file set (as-written, then runtime-extension swaps, then extensionless completion, then /index.*).

nodes.id: test:test/resolution.test.ts::drops imports of files that do not exist; keeps ext-swapped, as-written, and index targets
nodes.label: test/resolution.test.ts::drops imports of files that do not exist; keeps ext-swapped, as-written, and index targets
IF a relative import resolves to nothing on disk THEN the system SHALL emit NO edge for it.
never ingest your own output
IF a project contains a previous `revitify-out/` (or `graphify-out/`) run THEN the walker SHALL exclude it, so the graph never ingests its own report.

nodes.id: test:test/resolution.test.ts::a previous revitify-out/ run is never ingested back into the graph
nodes.label: test/resolution.test.ts::a previous revitify-out/ run is never ingested back into the graph
HTTP viewer + API, traversal-proof

WHEN the server runs THEN / SHALL serve graph.html and /api/{query,explain,node,neighbors, path,communities,stats} SHALL answer from the live index; static files SHALL be allowlisted to the three artifacts (traversal requests get 404 by construction); WHEN graph.json's mtime moves THEN the index SHALL reload.

nodes.id: test:test/serve.test.ts::serves the viewer and the allowlisted artifacts; blocks traversal
nodes.label: test/serve.test.ts::serves the viewer and the allowlisted artifacts; blocks traversal
test/serve.test.ts::answers the API routes

nodes.id: test:test/serve.test.ts::reloads when graph.json changes on disk (watch-next-door pattern)
nodes.label: test/serve.test.ts::reloads when graph.json changes on disk (watch-next-door pattern)
the 7 MCP tools

WHEN an MCP client connects THEN tools/list SHALL expose query_graph, get_node, get_neighbors, get_community, god_nodes, graph_stats, shortest_path — every call JSONL-logged; IF a node is missing THEN tools SHALL answer with an error payload, never crash.

nodes.id: test:test/serve.test.ts::lists the 7 graphify tools and answers query_graph + shortest_path
nodes.label: test/serve.test.ts::lists the 7 graphify tools and answers query_graph + shortest_path
skill + self-checks
WHEN `revitify install` runs THEN the /revitify skill SHALL land in .claude/skills/; WHEN `revitify diagnose` runs THEN grammar loadability, cache, and graph presence SHALL report.

nodes.id: test:test/serve.test.ts::install drops the skill; diagnose reports grammars
nodes.label: test/serve.test.ts::install drops the skill; diagnose reports grammars
the redesigned graph.html stays offline

WHEN graph.html renders THEN the design-handoff template SHALL carry all four libraries (cytoscape, layout-base, cose-base, fcose) INLINED with zero external sources, the graph injected at the DATA INJECTION POINT (function-form replacement — minified libs contain $' substitution hazards), and `</script>` escaped in data; the original canvas viewer SHALL stay available as graph-lite.html; docstrings SHALL surface as the additive `summary` node field.

nodes.id: test:test/viewer.test.ts::is fully offline: four libraries inlined, zero external sources
nodes.label: test/viewer.test.ts::is fully offline: four libraries inlined, zero external sources

nodes.id: test:test/viewer.test.ts::injects the graph at the DATA INJECTION POINT and keeps contract probes
nodes.label: test/viewer.test.ts::injects the graph at the DATA INJECTION POINT and keeps contract probes
test/viewer.test.ts::escapes </script> sequences in data

nodes.id: test:test/viewer.test.ts::still renders the zero-dependency canvas viewer via extraExporters
nodes.label: test/viewer.test.ts::still renders the zero-dependency canvas viewer via extraExporters

nodes.id: test:test/viewer.test.ts::docstrings surface as summary on their symbol (and stay as nodes)
nodes.label: test/viewer.test.ts::docstrings surface as summary on their symbol (and stay as nodes)
